title: santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. corsica
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. corsica, also known as Corsican gray santolina, is a shrubby perennial plant belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. corsica include relieving digestive problems, reducing fever, and soothing skin irritations, as well as having a calming effect on the nervous system. Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. corsica can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
